title: How to make a cup of tea DOI Forked
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"a4omh","text":"This protocol is part of the Record Keeping lesson within the FAIR in (biological) practice DataCarpentry course. Here we explore the perfect protocol to brew tea.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":29,"length":14},{"key":1,"offset":62,"length":29}],"data":[]},{"key":"a2uvk","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4hs3l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"58665F10A68411EBA77DE362B6D911DF","url":"https://carpentries-incubator.github.io/fair-bio-practice/06-record-keeping/index.html"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"27B24320A68411EBA77DE362B6D911DF","url":"https://carpentries-incubator.github.io/fair-bio-practice/"}}}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"5c9st","text":"Make sure the kettle is cleaned and the bottom of the kettle is free of lime-scale.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bpnro","text":"Should there be lime-scale, add small amount of 5% vinegar just covering the base to the kettle and leave over-night, or add about half a cm of 5% vinegar and boil the kettle. The latter should get rid of the lime-scale right away. Any type of malt-vinegar should do the job.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1l4vq","text":"Black tea is a liquid so elevated consumption might lead to increased bladder activity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2suc2","text":"Ideally soft tap water, Scottish tap water is perfect","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9r6u5","text":"Andrew James 1.8L kettle","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2ghmp","text":"Ceramic mug or cup","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ac8k8","text":"Black tea - Brodies Tea bag, 2g","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a3t6l","text":"Full-fat milk","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9cef","text":"tea spoon","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Fill the kettle with ~250mL of water, or until your min fill line and boil the water.
In the meantime prepare your tea bag, unpack it and place it in the cup/mug so that the tag is positioned outside of the mug.

Once the water is boiled, instantly pour the hot water into the mug. Ensure you fix the tag on the outside of the mug with your finger, to avoid falling in of the string + tag of the tea bag. Otherwise removal of the tea bag becomes more difficult.
Use the string/tag to remove the tea bag. Discard the tea bag in your compost for most appropriate recycling .
Add 5 mL = 1 teaspoon full fat milk to your tea
creator: Tomasz Zieliński
